title: Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024
subtitle: Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to [national estimates](#dod:ilo-national-estimates) and may not be fully comparable across countries.
caption:
Female employment rate, 1980 vs. 2024

Share of the working-age population who are employed. Figures refer to national estimates and may not be fully comparable across countries.

This scatter plot compares the female employment rate in 1980 (vertical axis) against the rate in 2024 (horizontal axis) for a selection of countries, with circle size indicating population. The data points are color-coded by region, including North America, South America, Africa, Europe, Asia, and Oceania. Employment is defined in line with the 13th ICLS and includes the production of goods for own use, such as subsistence farming. Most labeled countries sit above the diagonal line where 1980 and 2024 values would be equal, indicating an increase in female employment rates over the period. The United States, Brazil, and the United Kingdom cluster in the middle of the chart, with 2024 rates between roughly 50% and 60% and 1980 rates between 40% and 50%. Thailand and Burkina Faso show high 1980 rates, positioned near the 70% to 80% band on the vertical axis, while their 2024 rates sit around 60%. Conversely, the Bahamas is positioned far to the right with a 2024 rate near 90%, while its 1980 rate is lower, around 25%. Nigeria displays a 2024 rate near 80% and a 1980 rate around 35%. At the lower end of the scale, Pakistan and Bangladesh have 2024 rates between 20% and 40%, with 1980 rates below 15%. Malawi is an outlier with a very high 1980 rate near 90% and a 2024 rate around 35%. European nations like Italy, Spain, and the Netherlands generally show 2024 rates between 40% and 60%, with 1980 rates ranging from roughly 25% to 40%.
